note: Hand-maintained library of white papers, roadmaps and policy reports that remain relevant. why_relevant explains what each still tells you.

papers.title: National AI Plan
papers.publisher: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
papers.why_relevant: The Commonwealth's operating doctrine: three goals, nine actions — infrastructure, domestic capability + global investment, and diffusion of benefits. Every 2026 announcement traces back to it.

papers.title: Expectations of Data Centres and AI Infrastructure Developers
papers.publisher: Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
papers.why_relevant: The social-licence rulebook (energy, water, community benefit, sovereignty) that every build-out on the radar must satisfy.

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence Roadmap: Solving problems, growing the economy
papers.why_relevant: The founding document of Australia's AI strategy. Its three specialisation bets (health/ageing, cities/infrastructure, natural resources) still explain where public money flows.

papers.title: Australia's AI Action Plan
papers.why_relevant: The bridge between the 2019 roadmap and the 2025 National AI Plan — useful for reading how commitments evolved (and which quietly lapsed).

papers.title: Australian AI Industry Capability Report
papers.why_relevant: The best single census of the domestic AI vendor landscape: >50% of organisations using AI; Australian gen-AI consumer adoption outpacing the US and UK.

papers.title: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
papers.why_relevant: The 10 guardrails that became the de-facto baseline in Australian enterprise and government AI procurement, ahead of any mandatory regime.

papers.title: Safe and Responsible AI in Australia — Government Interim Response
papers.why_relevant: Sets out the risk-based regulatory posture (mandatory guardrails for high-risk AI) that still frames the legislative debate in 2026.

papers.title: Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government (+ AI Impact Assessment Tool & Procurement Guidance)
papers.why_relevant: If you sell AI to the Commonwealth, this is the rulebook for every tender and PO: Plan–Source–Manage lifecycle plus impact assessment.

papers.title: Powering the Cloud: Data Centres and the Future of Australia's Grid
papers.why_relevant: The clearest analysis of the binding constraint on the whole sector: grid capacity, and which geographies can actually host GW-scale campuses.

papers.title: Powering the AI Economy: Australia's $155bn Data Centre Boom
papers.why_relevant: The reference sizing of the pipeline: A$155bn+, capacity doubling to 3,100+ MW by 2030, ~A$26bn of new investment required.

papers.title: Seizing the Opportunity to Do Data Centres Right
papers.why_relevant: The counterweight read: what the boom means for the renewables transition, and the sustainability conditions states (starting with Victoria) are now imposing.

papers.title: Australia's Generative AI Opportunity
papers.why_relevant: Origin of the A$45–115bn-by-2030 economic-value estimate that still anchors most Australian gen-AI business cases.
